items.scenario: You signed up for a beginner orientation session at a new indoor climbing gym, but the confirmation email lists a start time that conflicts with a class you have that same afternoon. You would still like to attend a session before your first scheduled climb.
items.direction: Write an email to Ms. Calloway. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the scheduling conflict with your class
Explain why you cannot attend the listed session time
Ask if a different orientation slot is available this week
items.subject: Scheduling conflict for climbing gym orientation

items.scenario: You applied to be a teaching assistant for an introductory statistics course next semester, and the deadline to submit a supplementary writing sample has just been moved up by one week. You have gathered most of your materials but need more time to revise the sample.
items.direction: Write an email to Professor Dubois. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain that the new deadline is difficult for you to meet
Suggest a short extension of a few days
Mention your continued interest in the position
items.subject: Adjusted deadline for the TA writing sample

items.scenario: You live in a university apartment complex, and the card reader on the laundry room door has stopped working for the third time this month, leaving residents unable to access the machines. Several neighbors have already complained about the ongoing problem.
items.direction: Write an email to Mr. Kowalski. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how often the card reader has malfunctioned this month
Suggest installing a temporary backup entry method
Ask when a permanent repair will be scheduled
items.subject: Recurring card reader failure in the laundry room

items.scenario: You recently started a part-time job at a campus bookstore, and a family event next weekend conflicts with a shift you are scheduled to work. You want to resolve this without missing either commitment, since the shift starts partway through the event.
items.direction: Write an email to Ms. Whitfield. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the conflict between the event and your shift
Suggest a coworker who might be willing to swap
Ask about the store's policy for requesting shift changes
items.subject: Shift coverage request for next weekend
